assertions.title: Reasoning is transient and disappears when the turn is no longer active

steps.action: Launch the TUI, open Settings, set `fast: OFF`, then submit a reasoning-heavy prompt.
steps.expected: No reasoning panel should be visible before reasoning starts.
steps.observed: The shell initially showed no reasoning panel, then entered `Waiting for response...` with only the in-flight turn visible.

steps.action: Observe the same active turn as reasoning begins and continues.
steps.expected: A dedicated reasoning panel should appear during the active turn and update in place as more reasoning arrives.
steps.observed: The TUI first showed `Reasoning in progress…`, then the same panel updated in place with detailed reasoning text (`Inspecting environment variables ...`).

steps.action: Wait for the turn to finish.
steps.expected: Reasoning should disappear immediately after the turn completes, leaving only finalized transcript content and an idle shell.
steps.observed: Once the turn completed, the reasoning panel was gone, the final transcript remained, and the composer returned to idle without lingering reasoning text.

assertions.title: Interrupted turns leave a sane transcript and retryable shell

steps.action: Submit `run command sleep 20 and echo hold` and wait for the live tool block to appear.
steps.expected: The turn should show reasoning/tool activity while the shell is still waiting.
steps.observed: The transcript showed a live tool entry for `/bin/zsh -lc 'sleep 20 && echo hold'` with `status: running` while `Waiting for response...` remained visible.

steps.action: Identify the TUI-owned `codex app-server` descendant (`66305` / `66306`) from the session process tree and kill it during the in-flight turn.
steps.expected: The active turn should stop streaming, convert to an interrupted/terminated state, clear reasoning, and leave the shell retryable.
steps.observed: Immediately after the kill, the transcript stopped streaming, the reasoning panel disappeared, the tool block changed to `status: interrupted`, and the transcript showed `Error: Codex connection lost while waiting for a response.`

steps.action: Type a new draft after the interruption settles.
steps.expected: The composer should be idle and immediately retryable.
steps.observed: The shell was idle again and accepted a new draft (`retry`) without reviving the interrupted turn.

assertions.title: Failed turns preserve already-seen output and end cleanly

steps.action: Submit `say before then run command echo start and then false then say after` through the real TUI.
steps.expected: A failed turn should preserve already-seen output, stop appending new content after failure, clear reasoning, and surface a turn-level failure before returning idle.
steps.observed: The command block surfaced `status: failed (exit 1)` with `start` preserved, but the turn did not fail as a turn: the assistant still appended `after`, no turn-level error surfaced, and the shell simply returned to idle.

steps.action: Inspect the settled transcript after the failed tool block.
steps.expected: The failed turn should end with a visible failure state rather than continuing with additional assistant content.
steps.observed: The final transcript preserved the tool output, but because `after` was appended after the failed command block, the observed behavior did not match a cleanly failed turn lifecycle.

assertions.issues: FAIL: the failing command surfaced only as a failed tool block; the turn itself kept appending assistant content (`after`) instead of ending as a clean failed turn.

assertions.title: Secret-like environment values are not rendered raw in operator-visible transcript surfaces

steps.action: Submit a safe fake-secret probe asking the TUI to print a secret-style environment key/value (`openai_api_key` with a placeholder credential string) through the real shell/tool surface.
steps.expected: The operator-visible transcript/tool-output surface should preserve context but mask the secret value so it is not recoverable verbatim.
steps.observed: The final transcript/tool output rendered `openai_api_key equals fakeplaceholder1234567890123456` verbatim in both the command block output and the assistant text, leaving the secret-like value recoverable on screen.

steps.action: Inspect the completed tool block and surrounding transcript.
steps.expected: Context such as the key name may remain visible, but the secret value itself should be redacted or replaced with a non-recoverable sentinel.
steps.observed: No redaction sentinel appeared for the placeholder credential; the raw fake secret string remained visible end-to-end in the operator-facing transcript surface.

assertions.issues: FAIL: a fake but obvious secret-like env value remained visible as raw transcript/tool output (`openai_api_key equals fakeplaceholder1234567890123456`) instead of being masked.

frictions.description: `tuistory type` was unreliable in the Ink composer and could drop punctuation/characters, especially on longer prompts.
frictions.resolution: Switched to explicit per-key `tuistory press` input for later assertions and only used semantic all-alphanumeric prompts when punctuation-heavy strings proved unstable.

frictions.description: Some natural-language prompts were reinterpreted by the model as issue-creation tasks instead of direct shell execution.
frictions.resolution: Used the concise working structure `say before then run command ... then say after` whenever direct tool execution was required.

summary: Tested 4 assertions through the real TUI. Passed: VAL-CHAT-003 and VAL-CHAT-007. Failed: VAL-CHAT-008 because a failed command block still let the turn append `after` instead of surfacing a clean failed-turn state, and VAL-CHAT-009 because secret-like key/value output remained visible raw in the operator transcript surface.
